---
title: "LRP65: Adjusted model — independent baseline predictors of word-reading gain"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")


def _read(name):
    p = _here / name
    return pd.read_csv(p) if p.exists() else None


associations = _read("predictor_associations.csv")
prior_sensitivity = _read("prior_sensitivity.csv")
ses = _read("ses_sensitivity.csv")
```

## Overview

LRP65 is the Bayesian "interpretable estimand" follow-up to the LightGBM discovery
layer (LRP01). It answers the **static, between-child** question raised by the
descriptive work and the meeting: *do children who start (at wave 1) with more
letter-sound knowledge / language / blending go on to gain more in word reading?*

The candidate predictors form a correlated general-ability cluster (vocabulary–block
design $\rho \approx 0.63$; vocabulary–letter-sounds $\approx 0.4$–$0.5$), so a naive
"throw them all in" regression is multicollinear and "independent predictor" is
ambiguous. The DAG below fixes the estimand and the adjustment set **before** fitting.

## Estimand and design

::: {.callout-important title="Between-child association — match the design to the estimand"}

The estimand is the **mutually-adjusted between-child association** of each wave-1
baseline skill with subsequent word-reading gain, holding the other measured baselines
and $W_{\text{pre}}$ constant, *across children*.

Design: **one row per child** — $x$ = each child's wave-1 (T1) baseline level, $y$ =
word reading at the last wave conditioned on $W_{\text{T1}}$ (the gain framing). There
is deliberately **no** child random intercept: with one row per child the coefficients
are genuinely between-child. A pooled all-phase design with a child random intercept
would instead estimate the *within-child* association — a different question (handled by
the deferred cross-lagged model), so it is **not** used here.

These are **associations, not causal effects** (n ≈ 54). The randomised ITT models
(LRP52/55) carry the causal claim about the programme.

:::

![Causal DAG. Latent general ability `g` (dashed, not fitted) drives the correlated T1 baselines; the dashed edges from non-verbal MA, behaviour and SES to gain are the associations the model is built to test.](dag.svg){#fig-dag .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Adjustment set and predictors

- **Predictors of interest (standardised T1, per-SD):** letter sounds (`yarclet`), an
  equal-weight **language composite** (ROWPVT + EOWPVT + CELF), blending, age.
- **Baseline conditioning:** $W_{\text{pre}}$ (T1 word reading) via $\gamma_{\text{own}}$.
- **Tested covariates:** non-verbal MA (block design) and behaviour, entered to
  *demonstrate* whether they carry independent signal. SES is adjudicated in a separate
  complete-case sensitivity fit (it has notable missingness).

Each predictor's **adjusted** (mutual) coefficient is reported alongside its **bivariate**
(baseline-only-adjusted) association, so the shared-variance shift is visible.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

Fixed weakly-informative `Normal(0, 0.5)` on the standardised predictor slopes
(regularising under collinearity and n ≈ 54); the conclusion is checked against
`sigma` ∈ {0.3, 0.7} below.

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=32%}
![predictor_slope](prior_predictor_slope.svg){width=32%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=32%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Predictor associations (adjusted vs bivariate)

The headline result: each baseline predictor's standardised coefficient on the logit
scale, mutually adjusted, alongside its bivariate association. Intervals are 94%
(equal-tailed); `prob_pos` is the posterior probability the coefficient is positive.

![Adjusted vs bivariate associations](predictor_associations.png){#fig-assoc .lightbox}

```{python}
# | echo: false
associations
```

::: {.callout-note title="Prediction to test (not the assumed result)"}

Letter sounds and the language composite are expected to retain credible signal once
mutually adjusted; non-verbal MA, behaviour and SES are expected to shrink toward zero.
Wide intervals at n ≈ 54 are the honest result — do not over-interpret a single
predictor "winning".

:::

## Prior sensitivity

Adjusted coefficients under the headline slope prior and the `sigma` ∈ {0.3, 0.7}
refits — the which-predictors-clear-zero reading should be stable across these.

```{python}
# | echo: false
prior_sensitivity
```

## SES sensitivity (complete cases)

SES (mother's post-16 education) added to the headline set on the SES-complete subset
(reduced n, reported in the table).

```{python}
# | echo: false
ses
```

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
